reports.title: The CEO’s role in transformation
reports.summary: Transformations often falter due to internal dynamics that divide organizations, such as misaligned incentives, siloed efforts, and risk aversion. CEOs are uniquely positioned to address these challenges by tackling the root causes of collective-action problems and uniting their organizations around bold aspirations. Join Meagan Hill, Mathew Lee, and Kurt Strovink as they explore the role of the CEO in driving enterprise-wide transformation and embedding lasting change into the fabric of their organizations.

reports.title: How consumers are reshaping the future of mobility
reports.summary: Our recent survey reveals shifting consumer priorities, with rising expectations for affordability, sustainability, and technology and greater openness to new brands and transportation modes.

reports.title: How physician CEOs hone the double-edged sword of clinical training
reports.summary: The journey from physician to CEO has no prescribed path, but those who’ve reached the top reveal how they evolved the strengths and instincts forged in clinical training for enterprise leadership.

reports.title: Rewiring customer experience for the agentic era
reports.summary: As AI agents take on moment-to-moment decisions, leading companies are redesigning customer experience from predefined journeys to dynamic, cross-channel orchestration.

reports.title: Building expertise in the age of AI: Who trains the next generation?
reports.summary: As AI reshapes entry-level work, organizations should rethink how expertise is developed by integrating knowledge management, role design, learning, and coaching into a single system.

reports.title: Rewired takes: Practical people lessons for scaling AI adoption
reports.summary: McKinsey Senior Partner Brooke Weddle explores how companies are successfully scaling their AI programs.

reports.title: Is that AI agent worth it? Agentic economics and the modern operating model
reports.summary: As companies focus on spiraling AI costs, they need to make sure not to lose sight of the real prize.

reports.title: How AI is reshaping value creation in residential real estate
reports.summary: McKinsey’s Alex Wolkomir discusses how housing leaders can win with AI by improving customer experiences, redesigning workflows, and building trust between parties in the ecosystem.

reports.title: How a 180-year-old news institution prepares for its next reinvention
reports.summary: Associated Press CEO Daisy Veerasingham discusses AI, the economics of trusted content, and why preserving editorial independence matters more than preserving any particular business structure.

reports.title: Agents, robots, and us: How AI reshapes work and skills in Latin America
reports.summary: Most skills will still be needed—but how people use them will change as they work alongside intelligent machines.

reports.title: Author Talks: In Hollywood and in business, it’s cool to be kind
reports.summary: In Lead with Kindness, television writer and producer Melinda Hsu explores how so-called soft skills like empathy, mindfulness, and collaboration can result in better business relationships and performance.

reports.title: The real AI advantage
reports.summary: The next wave of value from AI will flow to those leaders who redesign business models, eliminate friction, and build organizations that learn faster than the competition.

reports.title: From adoption to impact: Three horizons of AI transformation
reports.summary: Most organizations are still early in their AI journeys. A new global survey reveals how companies can progress from individual adoption to enterprise-wide value capture.

reports.title: Cost versus value: Managing agentic AI system performance
reports.summary: Per-token pricing has stopped being a useful measure for what enterprises actually pay for gen AI. A conversation with David Tepper, CEO of Pay-i, delves into the new economics of measurement.

reports.title: From healthcare to health: Asia’s longevity opportunity
reports.summary: On average, people are living longer than before, raising a broader conversation about how to create an ecosystem that effectively combines the delivery of care with proven ways to prevent illness.

reports.title: Reimagining logistics pricing
reports.summary: Logistics players can focus on four pricing strategies in the AI era.

reports.title: Climate planning has prioritized floods. Heat demands equal attention
reports.summary: Summer in the Northern Hemisphere has gotten off to a very hot start. This has focused attention on the impacts of heat on health, infrastructure, and economic productivity—and how to best manage them. That is a good thing. While past discourse on adaptation has often focused on the impacts of severe flooding, our recent McKinsey Global Institute report on climate adaptation found that heat is likely to affect far more people and account for a larger share of adaptation costs, write Sylvain Johansson, Mekala Krishnan, Kanmani Chockalingam, and Annabel Farr in Fortune.

reports.title: The operating model advantage: Why AI winners are rewiring their organizations
reports.summary: The real AI advantage comes from redesigning how work is done and how decisions are made. Ultimately, success will depend on people and operating models supported by technology.

reports.title: From the C-suite to the boardroom: Christiana Smith Shi on what it takes
reports.summary: For executives, the developmental opportunity may be clear, but what does board service really entail?

reports.title: Why accelerated resource allocation matters in the age of AI
reports.summary: In an era of rapid AI disruption, the winners may not be the companies that predict the future most accurately, but those that learn, reallocate resources, and adapt faster than their competitors.

reports.title: Building America’s innovation engine: An interview with David Lebryk
reports.summary: The former US Treasury official shares how government can deliver in moments of crisis and how America’s public–private sector dynamic can help fuel its next era of growth.

reports.title: A microscope on small businesses: Chile’s productivity opportunity
reports.summary: Overcoming scale-related barriers and strengthening ties with other firms—both large and small—can help small businesses in Chile maximize their potential and narrow the productivity gap.

reports.title: How Covista is changing education for healthcare professionals
reports.summary: Chairman and CEO Steve Beard explains how the organization’s multiyear transformation has reshaped its purpose, structure, and culture.

reports.title: A dual transformation agenda for Europe’s chemicals industry
reports.summary: The strongest chemical company transformations in Europe perform a difficult balancing act: improving performance while radically reshaping portfolios, operations, and capital allocation.

reports.title: How Miro is redefining collaboration across the product development life cycle
reports.summary: Miro cofounder and CEO Andrey Khusid reflects on the company’s evolution from a digital whiteboard to an AI-powered workflow platform—and how AI is reshaping competitive advantage in software.

reports.title: The serial builder advantage: Why repeat innovators win
reports.summary: New McKinsey research explains why organizations that repeatedly build new ventures outperform those that approach innovation as a one-off effort.

reports.title: The real future of work in healthcare
reports.summary: Healthcare is in a productivity crisis that more hiring and more technology alone will not solve. The next era of care depends on making automation work through human–AI workflows.

reports.title: Building a more resilient industrial base may require $2 trillion in investment
reports.summary: As the United States approaches its 250th anniversary, intensifying technological competition and geopolitical risks have resurfaced a decades-old question with renewed urgency: Should the United States rebuild its industrial base, write Erik Kutcher and Shubham Singhal in Fortune.

reports.title: McKinsey Global Publishing’s 2026 book picks
reports.summary: We asked McKinsey Global Publishing and Marketing colleagues to share what’s on their bookshelves this year—here’s what they said.

reports.title: Lights, camera, algorithm: How AI is rewriting the rules of film and TV
reports.summary: Artificial intelligence is reshaping film and TV production from script to screen—but the biggest disruptions may not be the ones anyone is predicting.

reports.title: Closing the women’s health gap: The United Kingdom’s £36 billion opportunity
reports.summary: Closing the women’s health gap in the United Kingdom could result in ten more healthy days of life per year for every woman—and potentially boost the country’s economy by £36 billion annually by 2040.

reports.title: Betsy Nabel on physicians and women in healthcare leadership roles
reports.summary: Betsy Nabel, a leader and advocate for women in healthcare, reflects on the moves she’s made in her career to stay true to herself while creating lasting impact for medical students and patients.

reports.title: What to read next McKinsey’s 2026 annual book recommendations
reports.summary: McKinsey Global Publishing’s annual reading list is back, featuring compelling recommendations from a diverse group of leaders across the world. Our contributors have nearly 100 books for you to choose from.

reports.title: Suphachai Chearavanont: Bold moves in innovation-driven transformation
reports.summary: In striving for business growth while creating value for society, Suphachai Chearavanont has based his leadership strategy on looking beyond short-term gains to sustainable social impact and scale.

reports.title: AI in five industries: What’s now, and what’s next
reports.summary: The headlines are everywhere. The nuance is more elusive. In these video Explainers, McKinsey partners go sector by sector to parse what’s happening with AI now—and predict what’s coming next.

reports.title: Colocation data centers: The infrastructure race behind AI
reports.summary: Data center capital expenditures are surging as AI workloads grow, yet competitiveness increasingly depends on securing advanced accelerators, reliable electricity, and fast project delivery.

reports.title: Biopharma R&D: The evolving formula for discovery and development
reports.summary: Innovation after innovation is advancing the biopharmaceutical industry, enabling better treatment of complex diseases. The industry has historically made its home in Europe and the United States, but that’s changing.

reports.title: Talking with Daniel Marovitz, SVP of Fintech at Booking.com
reports.summary: Booking.com’s senior vice president of fintech describes how the travel-booking company has become the global platform of choice by managing complexity and easing customers’ pain points.

reports.title: Polyethylene: Widely produced—but not one-size-fits-all
reports.summary: Manufacturing polyethylene is a highly competitive business. Success depends greatly on access to feedstocks, yet investment doesn’t always follow economics.

reports.title: Semiconductors: Etching the new map of strategic supply
reports.summary: As geopolitics shift, more countries are wooing semiconductor manufacturers to enhance resilience. At the same time, demand for advanced semiconductors continues to grow.

reports.title: Solar power: From intermittent sunshine to reliable power
reports.summary: Solar photovoltaic projects with battery energy storage systems are moving beyond low-cost generation by transforming intermittent output into more reliable, higher-value electricity.

reports.title: Batteries: Scaling the engine of electrification
reports.summary: Demand for batteries is surging as electric vehicles and storage grow, but competitiveness depends on securing materials, ramping up gigafactories, and building the local ecosystems needed to compete.

reports.title: Pharmaceuticals: Innovating and advancing around the world
reports.summary: The continued rise of monoclonal antibody therapies and demographic changes are powering growth in the pharmaceuticals industry.

reports.title: EAF steel: Beyond the blast furnace
reports.summary: Global steel investment is flat, but DRI-EAF production is gaining share in advanced economies.

reports.title: Catalyzing competitiveness: Where investment happens and why
reports.summary: Competitiveness has moved to the top of the global agenda, and investment is its indicator and its outcome. In the context of dramatically diverging investment patterns globally, companies and regions can pull seven levers to level up.

reports.title: From access to assets: How early-life wealth building can shape economic mobility
reports.summary: Investing in children’s financial futures can narrow disparities and expand opportunity. Different scenarios for the new 530A accounts, also known as Trump Accounts, highlight the factors that can shape its impact.

reports.title: Author Talks: Why we need a ‘ritual renaissance’
reports.summary: What if the antidote to loneliness isn’t technology but ritual? Traveling across 16 countries, author Bruce Feiler uncovered a quiet global movement helping people reconnect in an age of digital overload.

reports.title: Australia’s grid opportunity: Why it’s time to flick the switch
reports.summary: Australia’s energy transition has entered a new phase. Moving beyond “should we?” the question is now how to deliver an affordable, reliable, secure, and competitive energy system.

reports.title: Economic conditions outlook, June 2026
reports.summary: In early June, executives were more downbeat on the economy than they have been in years and reported divided expectations for the coming months. Energy prices loomed large, and companies have been making defensive changes in response.

reports.title: Automotive R&D: Charging ahead in EV platform development
reports.summary: Improvements in batteries and recharging, not to mention geopolitics, have increased consumer appetites for electric vehicles. Automakers are flocking to China for lessons in how to develop an EV platform.

reports.title: Nuclear power: A renaissance in the making
reports.summary: The explosion of AI data centers and the continued electrification of automobiles and other products have greatly increased demand for electricity, sparking renewed interest in producing nuclear power.

reports.title: “被投资人怒怼30分钟后，我更确定中国To B的答案” | 甲子光年
reports.summary:
AI岗位化，重塑中国To B市场逻辑。
作者｜田思奇
编辑｜栗子
饭还没上，争论先来。投资人坚持“改成美式SaaS，不然没戏”；张韶峰回了一句：“中国没有SaaS。”
这句“逆势”背后，是他多年在一线看到的常态：项目越做越长、系统越来越全，最后的KPI却没有人认领。到2025年，行业走到了新的人工智能拐点——企业还能为功能买单吗？还是为结果付费？AI应用必须从“工具”被推向“岗位”。
据「甲子光年」观察，作为少数率先下场者，成立11年的AI原生上市企业——百融云创，其破局中国企业软件桎梏的核心思路是：把AI当作“硅基员工”。只有把考核指标与业务目标挂钩，让AI真正承担岗位KPI，AI才能在To

reports.title: 离开小米后，他赌耳机才是AI硬件的最优解
reports.summary:
AI硬件真正的分水岭，是Agent OS。
作者｜刘杨楠
编辑｜栗子
耳机盒是否只能作为充电配件？AI硬件上摄像头拍摄的内容，究竟是给人看，还是给设备“看”？手表在一套AI系统中的意义，到底是什么？
在光帆科技Lightwear出现之前，这些问题几乎没有被认真讨论过。但在技术史上，这类问题往往并不新鲜，它们通常出现在范式切换前夜。
苹果创立初期，乔布斯曾坚定认同一句话：“真正重视软件的人，最终必须自己制造硬件。”后来被反复验证的事实是，苹果创造的颠覆性，并不来自形态的出奇制胜，而是让软件与硬件像齿轮一样严密咬合，从而形成不可复制的使用体验。软件是核心，硬件是通向体验的入口，具体形态则是流动的。

reports.title: 甲小姐对话OpenAI科学家：每个人都在扎堆，要想办法与众不同
reports.summary:
把视野缩小到“要像OpenAI那样”是很危险的，你完全可以从OpenAI中得到启发，走得更远，走得更不同。
作者｜甲小姐 刘杨楠
编辑｜王博
“这是计划的一部分。”
这是《三体Ⅱ：黑暗森林》中，面壁者为了掩饰自己的真实计划而对外说的一句话。无论面壁者做的事情有多么荒唐，都可以被解读为“这是计划的一部分”。人们愿意相信“瞒过智子，对抗三体人”的伟大壮举是可以被计划的。
在约定俗成的社会理念中，每开始一项工作，人们总是习惯性地“确立目标-制定计划-执行计划-完成目标”。这套流程意味着明确的方向和有效的结果。
但是，做好计划真的是“万灵药”吗？
来自OpenAI的科学家肯尼斯·斯坦利（Kenneth

reports.title: 甲小姐对话黄伟：一边“找死”，一边活下去
reports.summary:
13年创业上市路，AI“老兵”的长期主义与现实生存法则。
作者｜甲小姐
创业13年，筹备上市5年，审核博弈649天——云知声创始人黄伟的故事像一面镜子，照见了中国AI企业的生存真相：在资本浪潮与技术理想之间，“留在牌桌”需要勇气，更需要耐力。
在AI从“听音识人”到“通用智能”的十余年跋涉里，云知声是一家不够喧哗的公司。它没有站在算法竞赛最热闹的讲台上，也没有卷入千亿补贴的算力战场。
更多人关注的是云知声上市之路的波折：
- 2020年11月：首次向上交所科创板递交招股书，后于2021年2月主动撤回。 
- 2023年6月：首次向港交所递交招股书。 
- 2024年3月：更新招股书，再次向港交

reports.title: 胡峥楠就任小米汽车CTO后首次受访：我的第一要务是重新学习
reports.summary:
一位造车老兵和一家科技大厂，如何在不确定中重建自己的技术方法论？
2026北京车展，小米汽车的展台依然是人流最密集的区域之一——在媒体日的第一天（4月24日），这里就排起了长队。
一边是新一代SU7交付提速、订单持续走高，截至4月23日，新一代SU7锁单超过60000台；一边是产品线外扩，全新车型小米YU7 GT，将于5月底上市发布。另外，小米汽车的首款概念车Xiaomi Vision Gran Turismo首次在国内进行了展示。
五年时间，小米从“造车新玩家”变成“必须被认真对待的对手”，而这一次，它试图讲的，不再只是某一款车，而是一整套更大的能力：高性能产品线开始成型，位于德国慕尼黑的小

reports.title: DeepSeek还有多少个“郭达雅”？扒完27篇论文，我们发现了一群“多边形战士”
reports.summary:
79位跨方向骨干、87%的初代留存率，和一套让年轻人围绕问题自由组队的机制。
过去一年，围绕DeepSeek的人才流动消息一直没有停。从早期罗福莉离职，到初代大模型作者王炳宣、多模态骨干阮翀、R1核心作者郭达雅，相继跳槽。
核心作者接连被挖，DeepSeek的技术壁垒会不会松动？
我们决定换一种方式来看这个问题。
我们用Codex和Python，梳理了DeepSeek近两年发布的27篇核心论文和技术报告，逐篇拆解署名作者。对DeepSeek V2、V3、V3.2、V4这类可拆分角色的大型技术报告，只保留Research & Engineering名单；其余论文使用原始署名名单。最终，得到一份包

reports.title: “人机共生，产需共融” 2026世界机器人大会新闻发布会在京召开
reports.summary:
2026世界机器人大会新闻发布会
徐晓兰讲话
徐晓兰表示，习近平总书记在2014年两院院士大会上深刻指出，“机器人是‘制造业皇冠顶端的明珠’，其研发、制造、应用是衡量一个国家科技创新和高端制造业水平的重要标志。”为贯彻落实总书记重要讲话精神，在工业和信息化部、北京市人民政府、中国科协的大力支持下，中国电子学会于2015年11月在京举办了首届世界机器人大会。习近平总书记在致首届大会的贺信中强调，“我们愿加强同各国科技界、产业界的合作，推动机器人科技研发和产业化进程，使机器人科技及其产品更好为推动发展、造福人民服务。”这为我国加快发展机器人产业指明了方向、提供了遵循。

reports.title: 机器人“真干活”，为什么宁德时代选择了银河通用？
reports.summary:
机器人进厂只是开始，银河通用与宁德时代要做的是交付闭环。
作者｜王博
宁王又有大动作。
据宁德时代官方微信公众号消息，近日，宁德时代与银河通用机器人正式签署全球战略合作协议。双方将聚焦智慧产线升级，推动具身智能机器人在全球市场的拓展以及规模化应用，并联合制定全球首个具身智能后市场服务标准。
宁德时代与银河通用机器人正式签署全球战略合作协议，图片来源：宁德时代
作为目前唯一获宁德时代直接投资的具身智能企业，银河通用再度彰显领先实力——具身智能重载机器人Galbot S1不仅是唯一通过宁德时代验收的机器人，其更已在其电池工厂“零遥操”自主作业，常态化融入真实生产流程。

reports.title: 张一甲：2025人工智能产业30条判断
reports.summary:
2025人工智能产业30条判断
1. 过去一年，AI发生了什么？
2. 此刻：轰然成势，万象归一
2.1 轰然成势，万象归一
2.2 “轰然成势”，是能量的临界；“万象归一”，是秩序的自觉
3. 定义：“默认式AI（Default AI）”
3.1 概念定义：默认式AI（Default AI）
“默认式AI”（Default AI）：无需用户主动选择或感知，默认存在、默认开启的人工智能形态。
3.2 默认即智能：智能成为常态，AI走向日用而不觉
3.3 默认式AI特征：关闭才是例外，不用才是例外，不会才是例外
3.4 AI社会化三阶段：工具式AI→伴随式AI→默认式AI
4. 2025年全球人

reports.title: 2025中国Data&AI数据基础设施白皮书
reports.summary:
数据应用与人工智能从相互独立到逐渐融合，正走向一体化
• 在数据作为生产要素被广泛认知后，数据应用和人工智能加速融合。高质量数据集的积累加快了大模型的成熟，大模型又反过来让各组织机构更好地发挥数据要素的生产力，开始从深度融合走向一体化。
数据应用与人工智能的融合，推动架构与能力实现阶段跃迁
• 第一阶段的感知智能主要依赖分类的非结构化数据，第二阶段生成式AI推动多类混合数据的治理体系发展完善，到了第三阶段的Agentic AI则对多模态数据融合与动态调度提出更高要求。数据应用与人工智能技术正从“AI+数据”的松散组合迈向“数据即AI、AI即平台”的深度融合阶段。
• 新阶段需要一个AI原生的、
